narrative: Jindal Stainless is the strongest overall performer in FY2025. It leads on total energy at 32.05 million GJ, non-renewable energy at 30.74 million GJ, non-renewable electricity at 3.06 million GJ, non-renewable fuel at 27.68 million GJ and physical intensity at 16.36 GJ/tcs. JSW leads on renewable energy and renewable electricity, reaching 3.23 million GJ and 3.06 million GJ respectively. SAIL improved materially in total energy and physical intensity, reaching 519.43 million GJ and 27 GJ/tcs, supported by disclosed CDQ, TRT, CDI, waste heat recovery, walking beam furnaces, renewable capacity plans and green hydrogen pilots. Tata’s FY2025 position is mixed but weak on the most important intensity metric: it ranks 4th on physical energy intensity at 28.3 GJ/tcs and 4th on total energy. Tata’s renewable programme is strategically relevant, but peers are currently converting renewable procurement and process-efficiency measures into better disclosed energy outcomes faster.
